originalTitle: US justice department launches criminal investigation into Trump accuser E Jean Carroll
summary: The case centres on whether the former columnist lied about the funding she received for her civil lawsuit against the US president.
content:
The US Department of Justice (DOJ) has opened a criminal investigation into writer E Jean Carroll, who accused President Donald Trump of sexual assault, CBS News, the BBC's US partner, has reported.

The investigation is looking into whether Carroll committed perjury in connection with civil cases she brought against Trump.

Carroll, a former magazine columnist, accused Trump of sexual assault and defamation, and successfully sued him in two cases. Both judgements were upheld on appeal, but Trump has since asked the Supreme Court to overturn the first of them.

He has denied the accusations. The BBC has contacted Carroll's lawyer for comment. The DOJ declined to comment.

In 2023, a jury found Trump liable for sexual assault. He was also found liable for defamation for comments he made the previous year in a post on Truth Social.

In that post, he denied Carroll's claim that she had been attacked by him in the mid-1990s in a New York department store dressing room.

A second lawsuit in 2024 found Trump again liable for defamation in connection with comments he made about Carroll in 2019, in which he accused her of making up the claims against him to sell a book.

Trump has appealed to the US Supreme Court to overturn the first judgement, for which he was ordered to pay $5m (£3.7m) to Carroll. He has also vowed to do the same with the other case, in which Carroll was awarded $83m.

The new criminal case is looking into whether Carroll lied when she said in a 2022 deposition that she received no outside funding for her civil lawsuit against Trump, a source told CBS.

It was revealed in legal papers first filed by Trump's lawyers in 2023 that the co-founder of LinkedIn, Reid Hoffman, had helped to pay for some of Carroll's legal fees and expenses.

The issue was brought up during the case's appeal, and the court found that Carroll had "plausibly represented" in her deposition "that she had forgotten about the limited outside funding counsel obtained".

The "additional discovery... showed that Ms Carroll simply was not involved in the matter of who was or was not funding her litigation costs," the US Court of Appeals for the Second District continued in a 2024 ruling.

The investigation itself does not necessarily mean the DOJ will indict, charge, or arrest Carroll, Dmitriy Shakhnevich, an attorney and professor at the John Jay College of Criminal Justice, told the BBC.

He said it was still "rare" for the federal government "to prosecute an isolated perjury charge arising out of a civil case".

Generally, perjury is not "terribly difficult" to prove because it refers to a statement made under oath.

"[The DOJ is] going to have to prove that [Carroll] willingly, knowingly lied," he said. "And I don't know that that's a standard that can be met here."

The new investigation is being led by the US Attorney's Office for the Northern District of Illinois, one source told CBS.

CNN, which first reported the story, reported that while Carroll's deposition took place in New York, one of the individuals who helped cover some of Carroll's legal fees, Hoffman, has a non-profit organisation based in Chicago.

The BBC has also approached Hoffman's non-profit for comment.

Acting US Attorney General Todd Blanche, who personally represented Trump in the appeal cases against Carroll, is recused from the case, a source told CBS.

Since returning to office last year, Trump has repeatedly called on the DOJ to prosecute a range of his adversaries.

The DOJ also recently created a $1.8bn fund to pay individuals deemed to have been unfairly investigated during the tenure of previous presidents. But some members of Trump's own Republican Party have joined opposition Democrats in condemning the plan.

One of the chief concerns raised by critics is about potential compensation for individuals who assaulted Capitol police officers on 6 January.

Trump asks Supreme Court to overturn E Jean Carroll verdict

Trump loses bid for appeals court to reconsider $5m loss to E Jean Carroll

Trump claims E Jean Carroll 'fabricated' sex assault
chineseTitle: 美国司法部对特朗普原告E·让·卡罗尔展开刑事调查
chineseSummary: 该案的重点是这位前专栏作家是否对她针对美国总统的民事诉讼所获得的资金撒了谎。
chineseContent:
据 BBC 美国合作伙伴哥伦比亚广播公司新闻报道，美国司法部 (DOJ) 已对指控唐纳德·特朗普总统性侵犯的作家 E·让·卡罗尔展开刑事调查。

调查正在调查卡罗尔是否在她针对特朗普提起的民事案件中犯下了伪证罪。

前杂志专栏作家卡罗尔指控特朗普性侵犯和诽谤，并成功起诉他两起案件。这两项判决在上诉后均得到维持，但特朗普此后要求最高法院推翻第一个判决。

他否认了这些指控。 BBC 已联系卡罗尔的律师征求意见。美国司法部拒绝发表评论。

2023年，陪审团裁定特朗普应对性侵犯负责。他还因前一年在 Truth Social 上发表的言论而被判诽谤。

在那篇帖子中，他否认了卡罗尔关于她于 20 世纪 90 年代中期在纽约百货公司更衣室遭到他袭击的说法。

2024 年的第二起诉讼发现，特朗普再次因他在 2019 年发表的有关卡罗尔的言论而承担诽谤责任，当时他指责卡罗尔编造了针对他的指控以出售一本书。

特朗普已向美国最高法院上诉，要求推翻一审判决，该判决要求他向卡罗尔支付 500 万美元（370 万英镑）。他还发誓要对卡罗尔获得 8300 万美元赔偿的另一起案件采取同样的做法。

一位消息人士告诉哥伦比亚广播公司，新的刑事案件正在调查卡罗尔是否撒谎，当时她在 2022 年的一份证词中表示，她针对特朗普的民事诉讼没有收到任何外部资金。

特朗普的律师于 2023 年首次提交的法律文件显示，LinkedIn 联合创始人里德·霍夫曼 (Reid Hoffman) 帮助支付了卡罗尔的部分法律费用和开支。

这个问题在案件上诉期间被提出，法院发现卡罗尔在她的证词中“貌似合理地表示”“她忘记了律师获得的有限外部资助”。

美国第二区上诉法院在 2024 年的一项裁决中继续说道，“额外的发现……表明卡罗尔女士根本没有参与谁为她的诉讼费用提供资金的问题”。

约翰·杰伊刑事司法学院的律师兼教授德米特里·沙赫尼维奇 (Dmitriy Shakhnevich) 告诉 BBC，调查本身并不一定意味着司法部将起诉、指控或逮捕卡罗尔。

他表示，联邦政府“起诉民事案件中单独的伪证指控”的情况仍然“罕见”。

一般来说，伪证罪的证明并不“非常困难”，因为它指的是在宣誓下做出的陈述。

“[司法部]必须证明[卡罗尔]故意、故意撒谎，”他说。 “而且我不知道这是这里可以达到的标准。”

一位消息人士告诉哥伦比亚广播公司，新的调查由美国伊利诺伊州北区检察官办公室领导。

最先报道此事的美国有线电视新闻网 (CNN) 报道称，虽然卡罗尔的证词是在纽约进行的，但帮助支付卡罗尔部分法律费用的个人之一霍夫曼在芝加哥拥有一家非营利组织。

BBC 还联系了霍夫曼的非营利组织征求意见。

一位消息人士告诉哥伦比亚广播公司，在针对卡罗尔的上诉案件中亲自代表特朗普的代理美国司法部长托德·布兰奇已回避此案。

自去年重返办公室以来，特朗普多次呼吁司法部起诉他的一系列对手。

美国司法部最近还设立了一项 18 亿美元的基金，用于向在前任总统任期内被认为受到不公平调查的个人支付报酬。但特朗普所在共和党的一些成员已与反对派民主党人一起谴责该计划。

批评者提出的主要担忧之一是 1 月 6 日袭击国会大厦警察的个人可能获得的赔偿。

特朗普要求最高法院推翻E·让·卡罗尔的判决

特朗普要求上诉法院重新考虑 E Jean Carroll 损失 500 万美元的请求失败

特朗普声称E·让·卡罗尔“捏造”性侵犯
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
